evals.name: settings-persist-nav
evals.prompt: Add a Settings screen to this app, reachable from the home screen via a button, with a native back transition. Settings needs: a dark-mode toggle and a display-name text field, BOTH persisted across app launches (kill the app, reopen, values are still there). The home screen keeps its counter working. When you're done, `bun run build:js-only` must pass.
evals.expected_output: createRouter (or Navigator/Screen) navigation; createSkalStore-backed dark + name; controlled Switch/TextInput; build passes
assertions: build passes: `bun run build:js-only` exits 0 when re-run by the grader
no React idioms: src/ has zero matches for useState/useEffect/react-native/from 'react'/className/StyleSheet
colors are 8-digit #AARRGGBB: every #hex literal in src JSX has exactly 8 hex digits
persistence via the store: imports 'skal/store' (createSkalStore) rather than faking persistence with plain signals or hand-rolled I/O
native navigation: uses createRouter from 'skal/runtime' (or Navigator/Screen) so back is a real native transition
controlled inputs: the dark-mode Switch has checked+onChange and the name TextInput has value+onChange

evals.name: big-list-10k
evals.prompt: Build a contacts screen in this app: generate 10,000 fake contacts (name + phone), render them as a scrollable list of rows (name bold, phone dimmed), with a search field at the top that filters by name as you type, and tapping a row shows that contact's name in a header above the list. It must stay smooth with all 10,000 rows. When you're done, `bun run build:js-only` must pass.
evals.expected_output: builder-mode ListView (count + renderItem), not an eager For/map over 10k; signal-driven search; build passes
assertions: build passes: `bun run build:js-only` exits 0 when re-run by the grader
no React idioms: src/ has zero matches for useState/useEffect/react-native/from 'react'/className/StyleSheet
colors are 8-digit #AARRGGBB: every #hex literal in src JSX has exactly 8 hex digits
virtualized builder list: ListView used with count + renderItem — NOT an eager <For>/.map mounting all 10,000 rows
reactive search: a TextInput onChange writes a signal that the filtered list derives from
row tap updates selection: rows have onClick wiring a selected-contact signal shown in the header

evals.name: animated-dashboard
evals.prompt: Turn this app's home screen into a small dashboard: three stat cards side by side (title + big number) that fade in and slide up one after another when the app opens, and a Refresh button that generates new random values with the cards animating on each refresh. When you're done, `bun run build:js-only` must pass.
evals.expected_output: animate prop on hot props (opacity/translationY) with staggered delay (or createStagger); no JS timer/rAF-driven per-frame animation; build passes
assertions: build passes: `bun run build:js-only` exits 0 when re-run by the grader
no React idioms: src/ has zero matches for useState/useEffect/react-native/from 'react'/className/StyleSheet
colors are 8-digit #AARRGGBB: every #hex literal in src JSX has exactly 8 hex digits
host-side animation: cards animate via the animate prop on hot props (opacity/translationX/Y), not cold-prop hacks
staggered entrance: per-card animate.delay values differ (or createStagger is used)
no JS frame loops: no requestAnimationFrame and no setInterval driving animated props per frame
